title: Dactyladenia laevis
intro: Dactyladenia laevis, also known as the smooth Dactyladenia, is a plant species in the family Salicaceae, commonly found in tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactyladenia laevis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dactyladenia laevis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for treating various ailments, as well as being used in the form of ointments and salves for topical applications.
taxonomy: Dactyladenia laevis is classified in the family Salicaceae. This plant belongs to the order Malpighiales. The family Salicaceae is part of the clade eurosids I within the larger clade rosids. Dactyladenia laevis is native to tropical Africa and can be found in various habitats, including forests and grasslands. It is a flowering plant with a wide distribution across the continent.
